consumers.current_behavior: SQLModel table attesting a proposal_id with a human decision (approve/reject/refer). No proposal_version_id, proposal_content_hash, decision_case_version_id, or scenario_version_id. Binds only to proposal_id.
consumers.authority_effect: Defines the database shape; carries no execution authority (authority_level=needs_human_confirm).
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation table for historical data and receipt replay. New writes needing version-bound decision semantics must use DecisionRecord/DecisionValidity.

consumers.current_behavior: ReviewEvent carries an optional attestation_ref linking review events back to the attestation they were created under. Docstring says attestation stays the decision of record.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only link; ReviewEvent never carries execution authority.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation_ref in ReviewEvent as a historical compatibility link. No migration needed.

consumers.current_behavior: Docstring: 'attestation stays the decision of record (approve/reject); ReviewEvent records annotation/archive/reopen/compare_mark/...'
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation only.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace 'attestation stays the decision of record' with 'Attestation is historical human review evidence. Canonical version-bound decisions will be captured in DecisionRecord.'

consumers.current_behavior: Decision enum (approved/rejected/referred) used by Attestation.decision. Represents a human review outcome, not a version-bound canonical decision.
consumers.authority_effect: Typing only.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Decision enum for historical Attestation rows. A separate enum may be needed for DecisionRecord.

consumers.current_behavior: Attestation is included in the StateCoreRecord union used by write_records and read-back typing.
consumers.authority_effect: Enables write_records persistence path for Attestation.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation in StateCoreRecord for existing data. When DecisionRecord is added, include it too.

consumers.current_behavior: Creates Attestation row + receipt file + ReceiptIndex. Records proposal_receipt_ref in receipt payload but Attestation row only binds proposal_id (no version). Includes P5 forcing gate for high-risk proposals.
consumers.authority_effect: Writes an Attestation row and receipt. Does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep create_governed_attestation operational for existing consumers. Add a separate create_decision_record command for version-bound decisions. Deprecate create_governed_attestation once all consumers migrate.

consumers.current_behavior: Return type of create_governed_attestation. Carries attestation (Attestation object), proposal (Proposal object), receipt_ref (str), approved_is_not_execution_authorization (bool=True), execution_allowed (bool=False). Does NOT carry a separate ReceiptIndex, attestation_id, or proposal_id field.
consumers.authority_effect: Return value only; no authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep for attestation receipt compatibility. Future DecisionRecord writes will have their own return type.

consumers.current_behavior: create_governed_review_event payload includes an optional attestation_ref. When a review event is written under an existing attestation, this field links them via ReviewEvent.attestation_ref.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only link; the review event itself carries no execution authority.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation_ref in create_governed_review_event for historical linkage.

consumers.current_behavior: POST /proposals/{proposal_id}/attest endpoint. Calls create_governed_attestation. Returns AttestationCreateResponse with attestation receipt. Active legacy-unbound write surface — not yet formally deprecated, but a future deprecation candidate after a version-bound replacement exists.
consumers.authority_effect: Active Attestation write endpoint. Remains non-execution but is not version-bound.
prerequisites: Attest endpoint can be flagged as legacy/deprecated.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep /attest endpoint for existing consumers (frontend attestation form). When DecisionRecord endpoint exists, tag this as legacy and redirect consumers.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets open_for_review = not attestations. A proposal is 'open for review' when it has zero attestations. Uses attestation existence as a binary review-completion gate.
consumers.authority_effect: Changes proposal review lifecycle state in API responses. Does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace open_for_review = not attestations with a query against DecisionValidity. During transition, use dual read: check DecisionValidity first, fall back to Attestation.

consumers.current_behavior: API response model exposes open_for_review: bool derived from attestation existence. Consumers (frontend, tests) use this to decide whether proposal needs review.
consumers.authority_effect: API shape contract; affects all consumers of GET /proposals/{id}.
consumers.recommended_change: Add decision_validity field to ProposalReviewResponse. During transition, set open_for_review from both sources. Eventually deprecate attestation-only derivation.

consumers.current_behavior: ReviewTaskLifecycle has state 'completed' derived from attestation presence. open_for_review field in lifecycle uses attestation absence/presence.
consumers.authority_effect: Drives review task UX; does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive ReviewTaskLifecycle.state from DecisionValidity instead of Attestation. During transition, both sources contribute.

consumers.current_behavior: GET /proposals supports status=open|attested|all filter. status=open filters to open_for_review=True. status=attested filters to open_for_review=False. Uses attestation existence for filtering.
consumers.authority_effect: API filtering contract.
consumers.recommended_change: During transition, include decision_validity_status in filtering logic. Keep 'open'/'attested' labels for backward compatibility.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets review_state = 'human_attestation_recorded' when open_for_review is False (attestation exists). Sets 'pending_human_review' when open_for_review is True.
consumers.authority_effect: Agent review surface state — affects agent context presentation.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive AgentReviewSurface.review_state from DecisionValidity instead of attestation presence.

consumers.current_behavior: Computes count of open proposals by filtering out those with attestations. Uses attestation existence to determine proposal openness for cockpit dashboard.
consumers.authority_effect: Dashboard display only; read-only projection.
consumers.recommended_change: During transition, count open proposals from DecisionValidity instead of Attestation. Fall back to attestation count for backward compatibility.

consumers.current_behavior: Queries all Attestation rows and builds attested_ids set. Each proposal in attested_ids gets queue status 'reviewed'. Drives the review queue UX.
consumers.authority_effect: Drives review queue priority and status; does not authorize execution.
prerequisites: All review queue consumers are identified.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids lookup with DecisionValidity.status query. During transition, merge both sources.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets review queue status to 'reviewed' when proposal_id is in attested_ids. Core queue classification logic.
consumers.authority_effect: Directly classifies proposals in the review queue.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids membership check with DecisionValidity status check.

consumers.current_behavior: Merges Attestation and ReviewEvent into a unified timeline. Each Attestation is a timeline entry with source_type='attestation'. Historical evidence display.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display; historical evidence preservation.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation in timeline forever as historical review evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add as separate timeline entry type.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets open_for_review = proposal_id not in attested_ids. Computes review_state fields from attestation presence.
consumers.authority_effect: Per-proposal queue item classification.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive open_for_review from DecisionValidity. During transition, combine both sources.

consumers.current_behavior: Uses attested_ids to exclude already-attested proposals from duplicate detection. Only considers non-attested proposals as potential duplicates.
consumers.authority_effect: Affects duplicate proposal detection and review queue display.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids filter with DecisionValidity status check.

consumers.current_behavior: Lists 'human_attestation' as a blocked transition for proposals that are not ready. Treats attestation as a transition gate name.
consumers.authority_effect: Gate naming; does not change attestation behavior directly.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep the transition name 'human_attestation' for backward compatibility. Future blocked transitions may use 'decision_record' instead.

consumers.current_behavior: non_claims includes: 'Human review is required before any decision of record.' Implicitly equates attestation with decision of record.
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim.
consumers.recommended_change: Rephrase to: 'Human review produces a historical attestation. Canonical version-bound decisions are captured in DecisionRecord.'

consumers.current_behavior: Docstring: 'Attestation stays the decision of record; review events are the interaction ledger.'
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace with: 'Attestation is historical review evidence. DecisionRecord captures version-bound canonical decisions.'

consumers.current_behavior: Two module-level non_claims tuples (lines 70, 76) both contain: 'Human review is required before any decision of record.' These are general authority boundary claims applied to agent proposal/review-note drafts, not explicit Attestation bindings.
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim in agent tool metadata — general authority boundary, not Attestation-specific.
prerequisites: AUT3 decision authority model is defined.
consumers.recommended_change: Review whether 'decision of record' here refers to Attestation or a future DecisionRecord. Align with AUT3/DecisionRecord terminology when available.

consumers.current_behavior: States: 'invariants: append-only; attestation is decision of record, not execution.'
consumers.authority_effect: Architecture documentation claim.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace with: 'Attestation is historical human review evidence. DecisionRecord is the version-bound canonical decision.'

consumers.current_behavior: Queries Proposal and Attestation, constructs attested proposal IDs, removes attested proposals, and supplies DailyBrief.open_review_count and open review content.
consumers.authority_effect: Drives daily brief open review count; read-only state gate.
consumers.recommended_change: Use version-bound DecisionValidity for current review state while preserving historical Attestation evidence.

consumers.current_behavior: Queries all Attestation rows, groups by proposal, and computes open_count, attested_count, approved_count, rejected_count. Uses the latest Attestation per proposal as the decision outcome for that period.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only annual review aggregation; no execution authority.
consumers.recommended_change: During transition, preserve historical Attestation counts but separately expose version-bound DecisionRecord/DecisionValidity counts. Do not silently relabel old Attestations as DecisionRecords.

consumers.current_behavior: Maintained API reference that documents the attestation endpoint and its current semantics. Defines the public contract for attestation consumers.
consumers.authority_effect: API documentation; authoritative reference for current attestation behavior.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation endpoint documentation. Add DecisionRecord endpoint documentation alongside it. Update cross-references when legacy endpoint is deprecated.

consumers.current_behavior: Documents 'human_attestation' as a review lifecycle state in the agent runtime reference. Defines that 'The draft should not become a human decision of record yet.'
consumers.authority_effect: Agent runtime documentation; defines lifecycle contract.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep lifecycle documentation. Add DecisionValidity lifecycle states alongside existing attestation states when available.

consumers.current_behavior: Renders attestation list on proposal detail page. Displays decision, attester, reason for each attestation. Pure display of historical data.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display. No authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation display as historical evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add separate display section for canonical decisions.

consumers.current_behavior: Renders attestation form (decision select, attester, reason) and POSTs to /proposals/{id}/attest. Active write surface.
consumers.authority_effect: User-facing write surface; does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation form for backward compatibility. Add separate DecisionRecord form when available.

consumers.current_behavior: Renders merged timeline showing attestations alongside review events. Each attestation entry shows source_type='attestation', kind, attester. Read-only historical display.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display; historical evidence.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation timeline entries as historical evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add it as a separate timeline entry type.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests attestation creation, read, and response shape. Locks current attestation API contract including open_for_review computation and status filtering.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: When DecisionRecord exists, add dual-read transition tests. Existing attestation tests must continue to pass for backward compatibility.
test_implications: Full attestation API contract test file.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests that agent proposal drafts interact correctly with attestation lifecycle. Verifies attestation presence affects review state.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: When DecisionRecord exists, extend tests to cover dual-read path. Keep existing attestation lifecycle tests.
test_implications: Full agent proposal attestation test file.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests Attestation model persistence, serialization round-trips, and StateCore reconstruction. Locks current Attestation schema.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation serialization tests for historical data integrity. Add DecisionRecord serialization tests separately.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests risk classification related to attestation decisions. Uses attestation decision state in risk evaluation.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: Review whether risk classification should use DecisionRecord instead of Attestation. Transition after DecisionRecord exists.

consumers.current_behavior: Registers attestation as a receipt-backed write in the governance write registry. Documents the attestation write surface for governance auditing.
consumers.authority_effect: Governance documentation; no runtime effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation entry in write registry. When DecisionRecord write exists, add a separate entry.

exclusions.reason: Extracts attestation_ref from context detail dict as a summary key for agent context pack building. Indirect data extraction, not a direct Attestation consumer.

Uses status='attested' for DomainMemory entries. Different domain concept, not proposal attestation.
